title: China-to-Bangladesh Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: BSTI Scope Check for Solar PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: BSTI is Bangladesh's national standards and conformity-assessment body. For a PV inverter shipment, the honest first step is to confirm whether the exact inverter, bundled plug, cable, switchgear, enclosure, charger function, label, HS route, or import instruction is in a BSTI mandatory certification or clearance scope. Do not treat every standalone PV inverter as blanket BSTI-compulsory without a product-specific official scope citation.
target_standards: Bangladesh Standards and Testing Institution Act, 2018
BSTI product certification / certification mark scope where specifically applicable
Bangladesh Standard or adopted IEC standard where cited for the exact product route
rows.china_equivalent: China factory files may include GB/T inverter reports, CQC or CGC voluntary certificates, IEC CB reports, Chinese labels, and domestic shipping papers. These are useful inputs but do not determine Bangladesh BSTI mandatory scope or import clearance status.
china_standards: IEC 62109 CB Scheme reports where held
rows.gap: Prepare a BSTI and customs scope memo covering HS code, model identity, AC/DC ratings, bundled accessories, importer role, labels, manuals, invoice description, and the exact Bangladesh source that makes any standard compulsory or buyer-required.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only where the exact product, bundled component, HS route, import instruction, tender, or buyer document places it in BSTI compulsory certification or clearance scope
mandatory_status.cn: China GB/T, CQC, CGC, and domestic component approvals are supporting evidence only and do not replace Bangladesh scope confirmation

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat BSTI as a product-scope confirmation gate; do not describe all PV inverters as automatically BSTI-compulsory unless an official Bangladesh source says so for the exact product route.

source.publisher: Legislative and Parliamentary Affairs Division, Ministry of Law, Justice and Parliamentary Affairs, Bangladesh

rows.requirement: IEC 62109 Safety Evidence for PV Power Converters
rows.target_requirement: IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the international safety baseline for power converters used in photovoltaic power systems and for inverter-specific requirements. In Bangladesh, IEC 62109 evidence may be requested by BSTI, SREDA project reviewers, utilities, EPC contractors, tenders, financiers, insurers, or buyers even where a public source does not prove a blanket mandatory whole-unit certificate for every PV inverter.
target_standards: IECEE CB Scheme where accepted by the Bangladesh stakeholder
rows.china_equivalent: China-origin inverter packages may contain IEC 62109 CB certificates and GB/T 37408 or NB/T 32004 reports. The Bangladesh file should map the exact model, critical components, software version, residual-current protection, labels, warnings, and manuals rather than relying on a generic China certificate title.
rows.gap: Build one identity and safety crosswalk linking factory model codes, sales SKUs, labels, CB certificates, test reports, critical component lists, manuals, packing list, invoice names, and Bangladesh project or import names.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary unless BSTI, SREDA, BPDB / DPDC, tender, insurer, financier, project owner, buyer, or customs route makes IEC 62109 or CB evidence required
mandatory_status.cn: China evidence remains separate and does not replace IEC 62109 where Bangladesh stakeholders require it

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109 is the core safety-evidence package when requested, but it is not by itself a Bangladesh grid approval, import clearance, or BSTI scope decision.

rows.requirement: China GB/T 37408 / GB/T 19964 Baseline Versus Bangladesh Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Bangladesh acceptance is controlled by the relevant BSTI scope decision, SREDA net-metering route, BPDB / DPDC or other utility approval, customs route, buyer requirements, and project documents. China GB/T reports are useful technical baselines, but they are not Bangladesh standards unless accepted by the Bangladesh route for the exact shipment or project.
target_standards: BSTI scope decision for the exact product route
SREDA net-metering and renewable-energy project route where applicable
BPDB / DPDC / distribution utility site-specific grid requirements
rows.china_equivalent: The common China baseline includes GB/T 37408 for photovoltaic inverter technical requirements, GB/T 19964 for photovoltaic power-station grid connection, NB/T 32004 for PV grid-connected inverter requirements, and voluntary CQC or CGC certification where held.
china_standards: CQC / CGC voluntary certification rules where applicable
rows.gap: Create a standards crosswalk from each Chinese clause and test report to IEC 62109 safety evidence, Bangladesh utility protection settings, labels, manuals, importer declarations, and the exact buyer or project specification.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only through Bangladesh import, utility, project, tender, or buyer routes that require the mapped evidence
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T and NB/T evidence is China-market or voluntary/project evidence and is not a Bangladesh approval by itself

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Use China GB/T files as source evidence for a Bangladesh crosswalk; do not present them as automatic BSTI, SREDA, BPDB, DPDC, or customs acceptance.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation Standard Public Service Platform (China)

rows.target_requirement: A Bangladesh-ready file should combine product identity, IEC 62109 safety evidence where requested, BSTI scope memo, SREDA or utility application materials, 50 Hz setting sheet, English manuals and labels, importer details, and project-specific declarations. The exact required set depends on whether the shipment is for retail sale, EPC procurement, rooftop net metering, utility-scale generation, or replacement equipment.
target_standards: BSTI product and standards scope where applicable
SREDA Net Metering Guideline 2018 where applicable
BPDB / DPDC / distribution utility project requirements where applicable
IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 where requested
rows.china_equivalent: A China export file may already include IEC CB certificates, GB/T reports, factory ISO certificates, labels, manuals, and utility-setting screenshots. These should be normalized into a Bangladesh file with consistent model names and local project references.
rows.gap: Resolve naming mismatches across factory model, export invoice, label, CB certificate, GB/T report, SREDA form, utility application, and project bill of materials before relying on any certificate.

mandatory_status.target: Project-specific; mandatory where the importer, BSTI, SREDA, BPDB / DPDC, tender, buyer, financier, insurer, or customs route requires the document set
mandatory_status.cn: China export documentation is a starting point, not a Bangladesh approval file until mapped and accepted

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] The deliverable for Bangladesh should be a route-specific file, not a generic bundle of China certificates.

source.publisher: Power Division, Ministry of Power, Energy and Mineral Resources, Bangladesh

rows.requirement: Bangladesh 50 Hz Grid Frequency and Protection Settings
rows.target_requirement: Bangladesh operates a 50 Hz power system. PV inverters should be configured and documented for the Bangladesh utility's nominal frequency, frequency trip limits, reconnection timing, voltage and frequency ride-through or disconnection expectations, and commissioning evidence required for the specific project.
target_standards: Bangladesh 50 Hz utility supply practice
Project-specific BPDB / DPDC / distribution utility frequency-protection settings
SREDA net-metering application settings where applicable
rows.china_equivalent: China also uses a 50 Hz grid, so base hardware is often compatible. The gap is not nominal frequency alone; it is the local firmware profile, trip thresholds, reconnection delay, anti-islanding behavior, and utility commissioning proof required in Bangladesh.
rows.gap: Keep a Bangladesh settings sheet showing nominal frequency, frequency and voltage trip points, reconnection delay, active/reactive power functions, anti-islanding method, firmware version, password or installer-lock control, and final commissioning screenshots or logs.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected operation where the Bangladesh utility or project documents require specific 50 Hz protection and commissioning settings
mandatory_status.cn: China 50 Hz compatibility is useful but does not prove Bangladesh utility settings

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Shared 50 Hz nominal frequency reduces hardware risk, but Bangladesh project files still need local protection settings and commissioning evidence.

rows.requirement: SREDA Net Metering Guideline 2018 for Customer-Side Solar
rows.target_requirement: Bangladesh's SREDA net-metering framework is the practical route for many rooftop and customer-side solar projects that export surplus electricity to the distribution network. The inverter package should support the net-metering application with model identity, single-line diagrams, anti-islanding behavior, protection settings, metering interface information, commissioning records, and any utility-required declarations.
target_standards: SREDA Net Metering Guideline 2018
SREDA renewable-energy project documentation expectations
Distribution utility net-metering application requirements
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic distributed-PV interconnection files, State Grid or China Southern Power Grid records, and GB/T reports can support engineering review, but they do not create SREDA net-metering eligibility or utility acceptance in Bangladesh.
rows.gap: The gap is project and utility acceptance, not only product testing. Prepare Bangladesh-specific diagrams, inverter setting tables, export-limiting evidence where used, meter compatibility notes, owner declarations, and commissioning sign-off for the distribution utility file.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for projects seeking net-metering treatment or distribution-utility interconnection under the SREDA / utility route; not a standalone factory certificate for all inverter sales
mandatory_status.cn: China distributed-PV grid evidence is not equivalent to SREDA net-metering or Bangladesh utility approval

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat SREDA net-metering as a project and utility approval route; a China grid certificate does not by itself prove Bangladesh net-metering readiness.

source.publisher: Sustainable and Renewable Energy Development Authority (SREDA), Bangladesh

rows.target_requirement: Grid-connected PV inverter projects in Bangladesh must satisfy the relevant utility's interconnection, protection, metering, commissioning, and operating requirements. Depending on the service area, the reviewer may be BPDB, DPDC, DESCO, BREB, or another distribution utility; BPDB / DPDC references should be treated as utility-route examples, not as universal approval for all sites.
target_standards: BPDB project-specific grid-connection and commissioning requirements
DPDC distribution interconnection and metering requirements where the site is in DPDC territory
Bangladesh utility protection and anti-islanding settings for connected distributed generation
rows.china_equivalent: China utility acceptance records and GB/T 19964 or NB/T 32004 test results are technical inputs only. Bangladesh utilities may require their own application forms, protection settings, inspections, witness tests, and metering acceptance.
rows.gap: Map each inverter protection function to the Bangladesh utility file: over/under-voltage, over/under-frequency, anti-islanding, reconnection delay, power factor or reactive-power functions, remote disconnection if required, and commissioning evidence.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected projects where the site utility requires interconnection approval, inspection, commissioning, or net-metering acceptance
mandatory_status.cn: China utility acceptance does not authorize Bangladesh grid interconnection

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat BPDB / DPDC or other utility acceptance as a site-specific grid gate separate from factory IEC or China GB/T reports.
